evidence: Great film. Captures the horror of Ted Bundy without buckets of gore. Very factual.
This is an excellent adaptation of Tarkington's book.
Great film. Not as good as Fargo, but terrific acting and script.
charming story with a real lesson.
Acting is stilted and lines bad. BBC used to rule in this area.
Great film. Better than Costner version.
Sets are authentic. Actors do a great job.
I do get tired of all the T and A which takes away from an excellent performance by Julia Roberts and Albert Finney.
Very intimate views of Dylan in concert which I have not seen at his ones.
This is my 2nd favorite Austen story because I too had to give up matchmaking.
Alice finally having to accept the fact that she will have to work for a living is so much more powerful.

summary: The user shows a strong preference for biographical and dramatic films with high production quality. They appreciate simple narratives that evoke emotional resonance, while their brand loyalty is low, indicating a focus on content over creators.
